commands.author: steipete

target.title: fix(browser): surface ChatGPT UI warnings
target.body:
## Summary

- Wire ChatGPT UI warning detection into both local and remote assistant-timeout paths.
- Surface a structured `chatgpt-ui-warning` error while preserving existing incomplete-capture and reattach metadata.
- Recognize the browser observer's plain `Response timeout` as an assistant timeout so the warning collector runs on the common timeout path.
- Limit detection to visible semantic warning surfaces, bound candidate/message size, deduplicate results, and redact email/token-like values before logging or persistence.
- Add browser and session regression coverage, browser-mode docs, and changelog credit for @derekszen.

## Product decision

Detection runs only after the normal assistant timeout and delayed recheck fail. It does not abort an active response early. The DOM scan is intentionally limited to visible alert/status/dialog/live-region/toast/banner surfaces; it does not scan ordinary conversation text or broad `error`/`warning` class matches. The timeout payload records the first classified warning and otherwise keeps the existing generic timeout.

## Authenticated runtime proof

Verified against the existing signed-in Chrome profile on ChatGPT (`/api/auth/session` returned HTTP 200 with an authenticated user shape; composer present). Controlled proof used the exact production detector and production timeout/session code:

- Ordinary visible conversation decoy containing `sign in` and `challenge`: 0 matches.
- Controlled `role="alert"` warning inside an `opacity: 0` ancestor: 0 matches.
- Same controlled visible warning: classified as `rate_limit` with selector/role/live-region metadata.
- A real `Error("Response timeout")` passed the production assistant-timeout classifier.
- Production `createAssistantTimeoutError` emitted: `ChatGPT UI warning detected (rate_limit): Too many requests. Contact [redacted-email] token=[redacted]`.
- The real browser session/CLI persistence path then logged the structured warning, marked capture incomplete for reattach, and stored `stage: assistant-timeout`, `code: chatgpt-ui-warning`, warning metadata, runtime metadata, and redacted diagnostic paths.
- Stored response was `{ status: "incomplete", incompleteReason: "incomplete-capture" }`; terminal output retained `oracle session ... --render/--live/--harvest` guidance.
- Controlled DOM removed, temporary ChatGPT tab closed, original selected tab restored. No prompt was submitted and no account state changed.

The existing Chrome MCP profile does not expose the classic CDP HTTP handshake required by standalone `--browser-attach-running`, so the live browser Runtime was bridged into the production timeout helper and real session runner at the `executeBrowser` boundary. This proves the changed detector, classifier, terminal, persistence, redaction, diagnostics, and reattach behavior without creating a model conversation; it is not a naturally server-produced rate limit.

## Validation

- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm docs:check`
- `pnpm test` (1,127 passed, 41 skipped)
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm docs:site`
- `pnpm test:packed-cli`
- `git diff --check`
- macOS SIGINT regression accepts both valid Node outcomes: direct `SIGINT` termination and handled exit 130, while retaining the slow-client cancellation assertion.
- Structured Codex autoreview (`gpt-5.5`): clean after fixes for ancestor-opacity visibility, warning-phrase consistency, and the common plain `Response timeout` classifier path.
target.author: derekszen

commands.reason: re_review already completed after this command

commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No narrow code defect remains for an automated repair; contributor-provided end-to-end runtime proof is the outstanding merge gate.; Cleared: The patch adds no dependency or execution-source changes, restricts DOM collection to bounded visible warning surfaces, and redacts common email and token forms before logging or persistence. (sha=d684ce891782cb462e797a513bf99e5ea1e46c00)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds ChatGPT UI-warning detection to local and remote browser timeout handling, persists structured warning metadata, and updates regression coverage, browser documentation, and release notes.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reduces the final browser failure to a generic assistant timeout after delayed recovery fails, and the missing warning propagation is directly visible in that path. The contributor did not reproduce a naturally server-generated warning, so this is not a full live reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No narrow code defect remains for an automated repair; contributor-provided end-to-end runtime proof is the outstanding merge gate.
